stem: An engineer takes 30 measurements of water flow rate using a flow meter. The measurements have a mean of 178.3 L/min and a standard deviation of 14.9 L/min. However, calibration tests reveal that a flow meter systematically overestimates the true value by between 1.4% and 7.0%. Estimate the true water flow rate and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 178.3 L/min. After correcting for the 4.2% bias: ≈ 171.1 L/min. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (1.4%-7.0%) and sampling variability: [161.2, 181.2]. Point estimate: 171.1 L/min.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
